name: when-profiling-performance-use-performance-profiler
description: Comprehensive performance profiling, bottleneck detection, and optimization system
capabilities: Profile CPU usage and identify hot paths with flame graphs
Detect memory leaks through heap snapshot comparison
Analyze database queries for N+1 patterns and missing indexes
Measure network request latency and connection pooling efficiency
Generate actionable optimization recommendations with code examples
Compare before and after benchmarks to validate improvements
author: Claude Code
securitySummary: All 269 static findings are false positives. The scanner flagged documentation examples, YAML frontmatter, and GraphViz diagram syntax as security issues. No actual malicious code present. This is a legitimate performance profiling skill for Claude Code.
valueStatement: Application performance issues are hard to identify without proper tooling. This skill measures CPU usage, memory allocation, I/O latency, and network requests to pinpoint bottlenecks and generate optimization recommendations.
limitations: Requires user to run application under load for accurate results
Cannot profile applications that do not have debugging symbols available
Some profiling modes may introduce performance overhead during analysis

useCases.title: Debug slow applications
useCases.description: Identify which functions consume the most CPU time and memory so you know where to focus optimization efforts.

useCases.title: Pre-deployment checks
useCases.description: Establish performance baselines before deployments and detect regressions in CI/CD pipelines.

useCases.title: Database optimization
useCases.description: Find slow queries, missing indexes, and N+1 patterns that slow down application response times.

promptTemplates.title: Quick scan
promptTemplates.prompt: Run a quick 30-second CPU and memory profile on my application at ./src to identify the top bottlenecks.

promptTemplates.title: Deep analysis
promptTemplates.prompt: Run a deep 5-minute analysis targeting all performance dimensions. Generate flame graphs and find memory leaks.

promptTemplates.title: Database focus
promptTemplates.prompt: Profile the database queries in my application. Find slow queries, missing indexes, and N+1 patterns. Suggest fixes.

promptTemplates.title: Compare builds
promptTemplates.prompt: Profile my application in standard mode, save as baseline. Then profile after my changes and compare to show any regressions.

bestPractices: Profile under realistic production-like load for accurate results
Use deep mode for complex applications with multiple bottlenecks
Always benchmark before and after applying optimizations to verify improvement
antiPatterns: Do not optimize based on guesses; always profile first to confirm bottlenecks
Avoid profiling in production without proper sampling to minimize overhead
Do not ignore p95/p99 latency metrics; averages can hide performance issues

faq.question: What profiling modes are available?
faq.answer: Quick (30 seconds), Standard (5 minutes), Deep (30 minutes), and Continuous for long-running monitoring.

faq.question: Does profiling slow down my application?
faq.answer: Sampling-based profiling adds minimal overhead. Deep instrumentation profiling has higher overhead but more accurate results.

faq.question: What languages and frameworks are supported?
faq.answer: Node.js, Python, Java, Go, and Ruby. Supports Express, Django, Spring, and other major frameworks.

faq.question: Can this skill fix performance issues automatically?
faq.answer: It generates optimization recommendations with code examples. You review and apply changes manually for safety.

faq.question: How are memory leaks detected?
faq.answer: By taking multiple heap snapshots over time and comparing them to identify objects that grow and are not garbage collected.

faq.question: What output formats are generated?
faq.answer: JSON reports, CPU flame graphs (SVG), heap snapshots, and optimization patch files that can be applied manually.
